title: Human Resources & Administration Manager
description: Urjatech is looking for a seasoned HR and Administration professional to independently manage people operations, statutory compliances, and facility administration at its cable and conductor manufacturing plant. The role requires practical knowledge of Indian labour laws, factory-floor HR, and the ability to maintain a safe, compliant, and productive work environment.
responsibilities: Ensure adherence to the Factories Act, PF, ESIC, Minimum Wages Act, Workmen's Compensation Act, and applicable UP state labour laws
Maintain statutory registers, returns, filings, factory licence renewals, and related permits
Manage end-to-end recruitment for blue-collar contract/on-roll workers and white-collar factory staff
Coordinate with labour contractors for worker documentation, verification, and onboarding
Administer attendance, leave tracking, monthly payroll, PF/ESIC challans, salary disbursement, and full-and-final settlements
Handle worker grievances, domestic enquiries, disciplinary actions, and industrial relations
Represent the company with factory inspectors, labour offices, pollution control board, and local regulatory authorities
Oversee security, housekeeping, canteen, transport, vendors, and general factory administration
Enforce workplace safety protocols, conduct drills, maintain incident records, and drive corrective actions
requirements: Minimum 5 years of HR experience in a manufacturing or factory setting
Cable or conductor industry experience strongly preferred
Strong practical knowledge of Indian labour laws including Factories Act, Industrial Disputes Act, Contract Labour Act, PF/ESIC, and Minimum Wages Act
Fluency in Hindi and working knowledge of English
Proficiency with HRMS/payroll software and MS Office
Ability to handle government inspections and regulatory audits independently
Experience managing contract and on-roll workforces of 100+ employees preferred
MBA/PGDM HR, MSW, or equivalent postgraduate degree preferred

title: International Sales Representative - Africa
description: Urjatech is expanding into Africa's power-infrastructure sector and is seeking a results-driven sales representative to drive export sales of XLPE power cables, AB cables, and high-performance conductors including AL59, AAAC, ACSR, and HTLS across East, West, and Southern African markets.
responsibilities: Identify and secure supply contracts with international and regional EPC firms executing OHTL and substation projects across Africa
Track, bid for, and win rural electrification and grid-expansion tenders from utilities and rural electrification agencies
Position Urjatech products for regional needs such as theft-resistant AB cables, AL59 conductors, and corrosion-resistant AAAC
Build and manage local agents, distributors, and channel partners across key African markets
Monitor infrastructure development plans, donor-funded electrification programmes, and competitor activity
Develop market intelligence to support Urjatech's Africa go-to-market strategy
requirements: Minimum 3 years of B2B sales experience in overhead transmission hardware, wire and cable, or electrical infrastructure products within the African market
Active network with procurement managers at African utilities, state-owned enterprises, and major EPC firms
Strong understanding of government tender processes, PVoC programmes, and trade finance instruments such as Letters of Credit
Fluent English; professional proficiency in French or Swahili preferred
Self-motivated and comfortable working independently in a remote-first environment
Experience with Indian-origin cable or conductor manufacturers on Africa-bound export orders preferred
Familiarity with AfCFTA protocols, KEBS, SON, SABS, World Bank, AfDB, or USAID Power Africa preferred

title: Tendering & Bidding Manager / Lead
description: Urjatech is seeking a highly motivated and experienced individual to lead tendering and bidding efforts for electrical and related products, with a primary focus on Indian government entities, DISCOMs, PSUs, and large public/private EPC contractors.
responsibilities: Identify, track, and analyse tendering opportunities from government DISCOMs, Central/State PSUs, and private EPC contractors
Lead bid preparation and submission, including review of RFQs, RFPs, technical specifications, commercial terms, and compliance requirements
Influence and negotiate favourable tender conditions through engagement with customer technical and commercial teams
Build relationships with DISCOM officers, senior executives, procurement bodies, and other key stakeholders
Lead commercial and contractual negotiations to secure sales orders and tender awards
Manage post-award activities including inspection nominations, dispatch instructions, BGs, performance certificates, and EMD refunds
Coordinate with on-ground teams for order execution, bill passing, and price variation letters
Track opportunities under schemes such as RDSS, IPDS, DDUGJY, and Saubhagya
Lead and mentor a team of 5-7 key account or bidding executives
Prepare official letters, customer responses, MIS reports, and market intelligence reports for Directors
requirements: Bachelor's degree from a reputed university
Minimum 4-5 years of progressive experience, with at least 2 years in tendering, bidding, and high-level liaison with Indian government entities or major contractors
Experience with State Electricity Boards, Central PSUs, DISCOMs, or major contractors such as L&T, Tata Projects, BSES, NPDCL, Adani Power, or Reliance Power preferred
Located in or ready to relocate to Greater Noida (West) / Ghaziabad
Strong understanding of Indian government procurement, e-tendering portals, and public procurement contract laws
Strong drafting ability for professional emails and official letters
MS Office proficiency, especially Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
Basic to good understanding of LT power cables and AB cables preferred
Track record of securing government tenders with SEBs/DISCOMs and yearly revenue of INR 20 crore or more preferred
Knowledge of BGs, EMDs, LCs, and government schemes such as RDSS, IPDS, DDUGJY, and Saubhagya preferred

title: International Sales Representative - North America
description: Urjatech is expanding into North America and is looking for a senior sales professional to establish and scale the company's presence across the United States and Canada. The role focuses on positioning UL-compliant power cables and advanced conductors with IOUs, public utilities, municipal utilities, renewable developers, and large EPC/transmission contractors.
responsibilities: Identify, qualify, and win new business with IOUs, public utilities, renewable developers, and large EPCs across the US and Canada
Map target accounts and build multi-level relationships with procurement, engineering, and project management teams
Develop a go-to-market plan for North America including target segments, key accounts, and revenue milestones
Position UL-compliant power cables and advanced conductors for grid reinforcement, reconductoring, transmission lines, substations, renewable evacuation, and interconnection projects
Work with technical teams in India on specifications, technical clarifications, type tests, sample approvals, and compliance support
Support customers on UL, ASTM, ANSI, NEMA, and related North American standards
Coordinate with internal teams and logistics partners on import/export requirements, tariffs, duties, and shipping documentation
Identify and onboard local distributors, agents, or channel partners where appropriate
Lead RFP/RFQ responses and drive the full bid cycle from pre-qualification to contract award
Maintain a structured pipeline and provide market feedback on pricing, competitors, standards, and upcoming projects
requirements: Minimum 5 years of front-line sales or business development experience in electrical, wire and cable, or utility infrastructure sectors within the US or Canadian market
Strong understanding of ACSR, AAC, AAAC, power cables, and related transmission and distribution products
Familiarity with US and Canadian grid/product standards including ANSI, NEMA, ASTM, UL; CSA familiarity is a plus
Proven success winning and growing accounts with utilities, renewable developers, or EPCs
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong negotiation and contract-closing capability
Hunter mentality with high ownership and ability to work independently in a remote setup across IST, EST, and PST time zones
Existing network with procurement, engineering, or project teams at major US utilities or EPC firms preferred
Experience introducing an overseas manufacturer or new brand into the North American utility market preferred
Willingness to travel within North America for customer meetings, trade shows, and site visits; occasional travel to India may be required
Must already have the legal right to work in the country of residence
